case_metadata.court: Armed Services Board of Contract Appeals
case_metadata.project_type: construction with steel work and containment systems

nodes.description: Government's motion for reconsideration of entitlement decision regarding 25 days of compensatory delay related to containment request modification P00009
nodes.source_excerpt: Government moves for reconsideration of entitlement decision regarding 25 days of compensatory delay related to Mod. P00009

nodes.description: Bilateral modification P00009 granted 50-day extension for government's review/approval delay of additional containment request
nodes.source_excerpt: Mod. P00009 granted 50-calendar-day extension related to review and approval of additional containments request

nodes.description: Welding subcontractor Garrison Steel caused delays and rework due to defective welds requiring repairs and reinspection
nodes.source_excerpt: Welding subcontractor caused significant loss in productivity due to rework required and repair nature of welding work

nodes.description: Concurrent delays from welding subcontractor issues overlapping with government-caused delays from September 2010 through project end
nodes.source_citation: section on Mr. Lowe's failure to consider concurrent delay
nodes.source_excerpt: Concurrent delay throughout period caused by welding contractor from September 2010 through end of project

nodes.description: Discovery of hazardous levels of chromium in spent blast debris that materially differed from conditions disclosed in RFP, where government represented waste would be non-hazardous despite known hazardous materials in paint coatings.
nodes.source_excerpt: Discovery of hazardous levels of chromium in spent blast debris differed materially from conditions disclosed in RFP related to risk of hazardous waste

nodes.description: Government's inability to provide DLA-approved hazardous waste containers in timely manner between 14 March 2011 and 20 May 2011, necessitating temporary storage solutions.
nodes.source_excerpt: Government informed Gaskins on 14 March 2011 that Mayport HWSF could not provide DLA containers, unable to provide them timely until 20 May 2011

nodes.description: Gaskins hired additional workers (increasing from 9 to up to 20) due to inefficiencies from transferring waste to temporary containers while waiting for DLA containers.
nodes.source_excerpt: DACA increased as-bid 9 workers to up to 20 workers necessitated by inefficiencies from transferring debris to temporary storage

nodes.description: Government claimed 32 days of delay triggering liquidated damages, but board found Gaskins entitled to 48 days of excusable delay preventing LD assessment.
nodes.source_excerpt: Government cannot support claim for liquidated damages based on 32 days of delay due to Gaskins' entitlement to 48 days excusable delay

extraction_notes: Modification P00009 events are procedural/contractual actions rather than physical delay events
The 'containment request' delay appears related to design/approval process but doesn't fit cleanly into existing categories
Welding issues could be classified as either subcontractor_default or inspection_failure - chose subcontractor_default based on primary causation
The hazardous waste container delay (n6) could potentially be classified as 'supplier_default' but fits better under 'material_shortage' as government was providing materials directly
The relationship between DSC (n5) and container delay (n6) is explicitly stated in footnote 3 but requires careful reading
Part 3 contains only administrative signatures and certification text with no substantive case events or causal relationships described
